chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for integrating Rheonics sensors with Siemens PLC S7-1200 using TIA portal software by installing a GSD file.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Rheonics Sensor Integration with Siemens PLC S7-1200 using TIA portal

Modified on: Sun, 15 Feb, 2026 at 12:39 AM

This section describes how to install the GSD file in TIA portal software.

1. Open a project.
2. Load GSD file (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter)
2.1. Select [Manage general station description files (GSD)] in the [Options] menu.

Figure 1. Screenshot of GSD file upload in TIA portal.
key_facts: The document describes the integration of Rheonics sensors with Siemens PLC S7-1200.
The integration process utilizes the TIA portal software.
Installation of a GSD file is required for the integration within TIA portal.
The GSD file mentioned is specifically for Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters.
The document was last modified on Sunday, February 15, 2026, at 12:39 AM.
diagram_labels: Siemens - C:\Users\rheonics\Documents\Automation\Profinet Integration with SMET\Profinet Integration with SMET

key_facts: A GSDML file named "GSDML-V2.4-Rheonics-SMET-20220321.xml" must be loaded and installed into the TIA Portal.
After the GSDML file is installed, the Rheonics sensor becomes available in the TIA Portal's hardware catalog.
Device configuration for the Rheonics sensor is selected from the device tree within the TIA Portal interface.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions with screenshots for integrating a Rheonics SMET sensor into a Siemens TIA Portal project using the Hardware Catalog and I/O device tree.
chunks.extracted_text:
4. From the [Hardware Catalog]
4.1. Choose the appropriate Rheonics sensor.
Figure 4. Selecting appropriate device from Hardware Catalog.

4.2. Select “Rheonics SMET” and add the device to your project.
Figure 5. Rheonics SME device in the I/O device tree.
key_facts: The document provides instructions for integrating a Rheonics sensor into a project using Siemens TIA Portal.
The process involves selecting the appropriate Rheonics sensor from the Hardware Catalog.
The specific device to select is "Rheonics SMET".
The Rheonics SMET device can be located in the I/O device tree under the path: 'Other field devices > PROFINET IO > I/O > Rheonics GmbH > SMET > Head module > Rheonics SMET'.
Figure 4 illustrates the selection process from the Hardware Catalog.
Figure 5 displays the Rheonics SMET device within the I/O device tree.
There is a minor discrepancy where the instruction text and UI show 'Rheonics SMET', but Figure 5's caption refers to 'Rheonics SME device'.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions and visual aids for integrating a Rheonics SMET sensor with a PLC using the Profinet topology view, showing how the sensor appears and how to establish a connection via drag and drop.
chunks.extracted_text:
5. Rheonics sensor will appear in the topology view.
Figure 6. Rheonics sensor as it appears in Topology View.
6 Connect the Rheonics sensor SMET to the PLC
6.1. Drag & drop the connection.
key_facts: The Rheonics sensor is integrated into a Profinet network configuration.
The Rheonics sensor, identified as 'rheonics-smet' and 'Rheonics SMET', appears in the topology view of the Profinet integration software.
A 'DP-NORM' module is associated with the Rheonics SMET in the topology view.
Connection of the Rheonics SMET sensor to a PLC (CPU 1212C) is achieved by a drag-and-drop operation in the software's topology view.

Figure 8. Establishing Profinet network by connecting Rheonics SME to PLC in TIA portal software.
key_facts: Figure 7 depicts a screenshot related to Module Definition-Module Info.
A green wire connects the PLC controller and the Rheonics sensor when the PLC and SME are connected.
Figure 8 illustrates the process of establishing a Profinet network.
The Profinet network is established by connecting a Rheonics SME device to a PLC.
The network configuration is performed within TIA Portal software.
